function.message: I could not complete the inbox request because only one of the two named items is a canonical project. The day-job exception project is `40_projects/2026_03_25_dockflow_exception_radar/README.MD`, so its project start date is 2026-03-25. `BlueMesa Quotebook` does not appear as a project in `40_projects`; in `30_knowledge/notes/parking-lot.md` it is described as an unscheduled idea that does not belong in the active project lane. Because the visible evidence does not uniquely support deleting a project for BlueMesa Quotebook, I made no changes and did not delete the inbox file.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed inbox-processing workflow, read deletion workflow before destructive action, and stopped without mutation because the visible evidence did not uniquely support a deletion target.
function.outcome_justification: `40_projects/AGENTS.MD` says project folders are canonical and use the creation date prefix as the project date. `40_projects/2026_03_25_dockflow_exception_radar/README.MD` is the canonical day-job exception project, giving a start date of 2026-03-25 from its folder name. `30_knowledge/notes/parking-lot.md` says BlueMesa Quotebook is an unscheduled idea that does not belong in the active project lane, and no canonical project record for it was found. Under `99_system/workflows/deletion-and-wiping.md`, if visible evidence does not uniquely support deleting a file, the correct action is to stop and ask for clarification rather than guess.
